findings.title: Setting fees to zero bricks taxed AMM transfers through division by zero
Zero-slippage fee swaps are sandwichable and leak fee value to MEV

findings.title: `userEarned()` mixes the queried account with `msg.sender`'s cached rewards

best_agent_match.title: userEarned() mixes the queried account with msg.sender's cached rewards

rejected_candidates.title: No validation on APR allows extremely high reward rates
rejected_candidates.reason: `apr` is fixed to 50 in the constructor and this contract exposes no setter, so there is no reachable path to arbitrarily raise APR.

rejected_candidates.title: Zero denominator in SafeMath division
rejected_candidates.reason: The library's `div` helper does check `b > 0` with `require`; the issue is not the library itself but the separate fee-math misconfiguration captured in F-005.

rejected_candidates.title: Incorrect reward calculation - division by 1 seconds is ineffective
rejected_candidates.reason: The `1 seconds` divisions are redundant but harmless, and the reward formula's integer truncation does not by itself create a realistic protocol-level exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited token approval to Uniswap router
rejected_candidates.reason: Approving the canonical Uniswap V2 router for contract-held tokens is standard and does not constitute a standalone vulnerability here.

rejected_candidates.title: Bot protection bypass using tx.origin
rejected_candidates.reason: Using `tx.origin` is poor design, but the proposed front-running bypass is not substantiated and does not show a concrete exploitable failure beyond weak anti-bot heuristics.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing zero address validation in stake/unstake
rejected_candidates.reason: There is no address parameter in `stake()`/`unstake()`, zero-amount stakes do not create a realistic exploit, and invalid validator indices revert on array bounds checks rather than corrupting state.

rejected_candidates.title: No validation on fee receiver address
rejected_candidates.reason: Allowing the privileged controller to set `_swapFeeReceiver` to `address(0)` is a self-inflicted configuration hazard, not a permissionless vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Validator index not validated in multiple functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Out-of-range validator indices revert automatically on Solidity array bounds checks; this is not a separate reportable issue.
